growth_pole.name: Growth Pole Theory
growth_pole.author: Perroux (1950)
growth_pole.description: Economic growth radiates from dynamic sectors/regions

cumulative_causation.name: Cumulative Causation
cumulative_causation.author: Myrdal (1957)
cumulative_causation.description: Virtuous/vicious circles amplify regional inequalities over time

unbalanced_growth.name: Unbalanced Growth
unbalanced_growth.author: Hirschman (1958)
unbalanced_growth.description: Strategic investment in key sectors creates linkages upstream/downstream

core_periphery.name: Core-Periphery Model
core_periphery.author: Friedmann (1966)
core_periphery.description: Dominant core exploits peripheral regions, creating dependency

export_base.name: Export Base Theory
export_base.author: North (1955)
export_base.description: Regional growth driven by export sectors with local multiplier effects

stages_of_growth.name: Stages of Economic Growth
stages_of_growth.author: Rostow (1960)
stages_of_growth.description: Regions pass through stages from traditional to mature economy

central_place.name: Central Place Theory
central_place.author: Christaller (1933)
central_place.description: Hierarchical settlement patterns based on market reach

agricultural_location.name: Agricultural Location Theory
agricultural_location.author: Von Thünen (1826)
agricultural_location.description: Land use intensity decreases with distance from market center

industrial_location.name: Industrial Location Theory
industrial_location.author: Weber (1909)
industrial_location.description: Optimal location minimizes transport costs (materials + labor + market)

bid_rent.name: Bid-Rent Theory
bid_rent.author: Alonso (1964)
bid_rent.description: Land prices reflect accessibility trade-offs from city center

market_area.name: Market Area Theory
market_area.author: Lösch (1940)
market_area.description: Optimal market areas form hexagonal patterns minimizing costs

spatial_competition.name: Spatial Competition
spatial_competition.author: Hotelling (1929)
spatial_competition.description: Competing firms tend to cluster at market center (minimal differentiation)

new_economic_geography.name: New Economic Geography
new_economic_geography.author: Krugman (1991)
new_economic_geography.description: Agglomeration economies from increasing returns and transport costs

industrial_district.name: Industrial District Theory
industrial_district.author: Marshall (1890)
industrial_district.description: Clusters benefit from knowledge spillovers and specialized labor

jacobs_externalities.name: Jacobs Externalities
jacobs_externalities.author: Jacobs (1969)
jacobs_externalities.description: Urban diversity drives innovation through cross-sector knowledge flows

spl.name: Systèmes Productifs Localisés (SPL)
spl.author: Courlet & Pecqueur (1992)
spl.description: French localized productive systems combining SMEs and local know-how

milieux_innovateurs.name: Milieux Innovateurs
milieux_innovateurs.author: GREMI/Aydalot (1986)
milieux_innovateurs.description: Innovative environments emerge from local synergies and networks

economie_proximite.name: Économie de Proximité
economie_proximite.author: Rallet & Torre (2004)
economie_proximite.description: Geographic + organized proximity enable coordination and learning

capital_territorial.name: Capital Territorial
capital_territorial.author: Camagni (2008)
capital_territorial.description: Territory-specific assets (hard + soft) determine competitive advantage

ressources_territoriales.name: Ressources Territoriales
ressources_territoriales.author: Colletis & Pecqueur (2005)
ressources_territoriales.description: Latent resources can be activated through collective action

economie_residentielle.name: Économie Résidentielle
economie_residentielle.author: Davezies (2008)
economie_residentielle.description: Residential economy (retirees, tourists, commuters) vs productive base

metropole_archipel.name: Économie d'Archipel
metropole_archipel.author: Veltz (1996)
metropole_archipel.description: Networked metropolises dominate, territories in-between struggle

cluster_diamond.name: Cluster Diamond
cluster_diamond.author: Porter (1990)
cluster_diamond.description: Competitive advantage from 4 factors: demand, supply, rivalry, support

endogenous_growth.name: Endogenous Growth Theory
endogenous_growth.author: Romer (1986), Lucas (1988)
endogenous_growth.description: Growth driven by internal factors: human capital, R&D, learning

learning_regions.name: Learning Regions
learning_regions.author: Morgan (1997)
learning_regions.description: Regions that learn collectively adapt better to economic shocks

innovation_systems.name: Regional Innovation Systems
innovation_systems.author: Cooke (1992)
innovation_systems.description: Regional ecosystem of firms, institutions, and networks for innovation

knowledge_spillovers.name: Knowledge Spillovers
knowledge_spillovers.author: Jaffe (1989)
knowledge_spillovers.description: Geographically mediated knowledge flows benefit local firms

smart_specialization.name: Smart Specialisation
smart_specialization.author: Foray (2009)
smart_specialization.description: Regions should focus on unique strengths through entrepreneurial discovery

creative_class.name: Creative Class
creative_class.author: Florida (2002)
creative_class.description: Regional growth correlates with concentration of creative workers

regional_resilience.name: Regional Economic Resilience
regional_resilience.author: Martin (2012)
regional_resilience.description: Capacity to resist, recover, reorient after economic shocks

evolutionary_econ_geography.name: Evolutionary Economic Geography
evolutionary_econ_geography.author: Boschma & Martin (2010)
evolutionary_econ_geography.description: Path dependence and related variety shape regional trajectories

circular_economy.name: Circular Economy
circular_economy.author: Ellen MacArthur Foundation (2012)
circular_economy.description: Closed-loop systems reduce waste and create local value

territorial_ecology.name: Écologie Territoriale
territorial_ecology.author: Barles (2010)
territorial_ecology.description: Metabolic analysis of territorial material/energy flows

product_lifecycle.name: Product Life Cycle
product_lifecycle.author: Vernon (1966)
product_lifecycle.description: Industries evolve through stages; production shifts to lower-cost regions

industrial_restructuring.name: Industrial Restructuring
industrial_restructuring.author: Massey (1984)
industrial_restructuring.description: Spatial division of labor reshapes regional industrial landscapes

technological_paradigms.name: Technological Paradigms
technological_paradigms.author: Dosi (1982)
technological_paradigms.description: Major technological shifts create windows of opportunity for regions

flexible_specialization.name: Flexible Specialization
flexible_specialization.author: Piore & Sabel (1984)
flexible_specialization.description: SME networks adapt better than mass producers to volatile markets

path_dependence.name: Path Dependence
path_dependence.author: Arthur (1989)
path_dependence.description: Historical events and choices constrain future development paths

lock_in.name: Lock-In Theory
lock_in.author: David (1985)
lock_in.description: Suboptimal technologies persist due to increasing returns and network effects

labor_market_matching.name: Matching Theory
labor_market_matching.author: Pissarides (1979)
labor_market_matching.description: Labor market efficiency depends on matching between skills and jobs

human_capital_theory.name: Human Capital Theory
human_capital_theory.author: Becker (1964)
human_capital_theory.description: Investment in education and skills drives regional productivity

brain_drain.name: Brain Drain
brain_drain.author: Grubel & Scott (1966)
brain_drain.description: Skilled worker migration depletes regional human capital

demographic_transition.name: Demographic Transition
demographic_transition.author: Notestein (1945)
demographic_transition.description: Population structure evolves from high to low fertility/mortality

age_structure_economics.name: Age Structure Economics
age_structure_economics.author: Bloom & Canning (2004)
age_structure_economics.description: Demographic dividend when working-age population peaks

social_network_theory.name: Social Network Theory
social_network_theory.author: Granovetter (1973)
social_network_theory.description: Weak ties bridge clusters and enable information flow

embeddedness.name: Embeddedness Theory
embeddedness.author: Granovetter (1985)
embeddedness.description: Economic action is embedded in social relations and trust networks

relational_capital.name: Relational Capital
relational_capital.author: Capello & Faggian (2005)
relational_capital.description: Value created through inter-firm relationships and collective learning

global_value_chains.name: Global Value Chains
global_value_chains.author: Gereffi (1994)
global_value_chains.description: Regions compete for positioning in global production networks

network_externalities.name: Network Externalities
network_externalities.author: Katz & Shapiro (1985)
network_externalities.description: Value increases with network size; tipping points matter

urban_scaling.name: Urban Scaling Laws
urban_scaling.author: Bettencourt (2007)
urban_scaling.description: Cities exhibit super-linear scaling in innovation, sub-linear in infrastructure

polycentric_development.name: Polycentric Development
polycentric_development.author: ESDP (1999)
polycentric_development.description: Balanced development through multiple interconnected urban centers

urban_sprawl.name: Urban Sprawl Theory
urban_sprawl.author: Brueckner (2000)
urban_sprawl.description: Low-density expansion creates inefficiencies and environmental costs

gentrification.name: Gentrification Theory
gentrification.author: Smith (1979)
gentrification.description: Rent gap drives reinvestment in inner-city areas

shrinking_cities.name: Shrinking Cities
shrinking_cities.author: Oswalt (2005)
shrinking_cities.description: Urban decline requires adaptive strategies beyond growth paradigm

social_capital.name: Social Capital
social_capital.author: Putnam (1993)
social_capital.description: Trust, norms, and networks enable collective action and development

institutional_thickness.name: Institutional Thickness
institutional_thickness.author: Amin & Thrift (1995)
institutional_thickness.description: Dense institutional networks support regional economic development

governance_quality.name: Governance Quality
governance_quality.author: Rodríguez-Pose (2013)
governance_quality.description: Regional governance capacity determines development effectiveness

territorial_governance.name: Territorial Governance
territorial_governance.author: Gilly & Wallet (2001)
territorial_governance.description: Multi-level coordination shapes territorial development trajectories

comparative_advantage.name: Comparative Advantage
comparative_advantage.author: Ricardo (1817)
comparative_advantage.description: Regions benefit from specializing in their relative strengths

world_systems.name: World Systems Theory
world_systems.author: Wallerstein (1974)
world_systems.description: Core-periphery relations at global scale structure development

global_city.name: Global City
global_city.author: Sassen (1991)
global_city.description: Command centers for global economy concentrate advanced services

glocalization.name: Glocalization
glocalization.author: Robertson (1995)
glocalization.description: Global forces interact with local contexts to produce unique outcomes

offshoring.name: Offshoring Theory
offshoring.author: Blinder (2006)
offshoring.description: Service jobs face offshoring pressure based on impersonal vs personal nature

just_transition.name: Just Transition
just_transition.author: ILO (2015)
just_transition.description: Decarbonization must address social impacts on workers and communities

ecological_modernization.name: Ecological Modernization
ecological_modernization.author: Mol (1995)
ecological_modernization.description: Environmental and economic goals can be reconciled through innovation

bioeconomy.name: Bioeconomy
bioeconomy.author: EU (2012)
bioeconomy.description: Bio-based resources and processes create sustainable value chains

renewable_transition.name: Renewable Energy Transition
renewable_transition.author: Jacobsson & Johnson (2000)
renewable_transition.description: Technological innovation systems drive renewable energy adoption

territorial_metabolism.name: Territorial Metabolism
territorial_metabolism.author: Fischer-Kowalski (1998)
territorial_metabolism.description: Socioeconomic systems have material/energy throughput like organisms
